description: Comprehensive neuroscience literature database for temporal processing in auditory perception - Supporting C⁰ cognitive foundation and R⁰/H⁰ integration
purpose: Scientific foundation for SRC⁹-Composer H⁰ temporal processing system

temporal_integration.description: Temporal integration windows and cortical hierarchy in auditory processing
temporal_integration.h0_relevance: Core mechanism for multi-scale temporal integration (50-400ms windows)

neural_oscillations.description: Delta, theta, alpha, beta, gamma oscillations in temporal processing
neural_oscillations.h0_relevance: Oscillatory basis for temporal encoding and prediction

predictive_timing.description: Temporal expectation and predictive processing in auditory cortex

interval_timing.description: Basal ganglia, SMA, and cerebellum in duration estimation

beat_rhythm.description: Beat perception, meter, and sensorimotor synchronization

speech_temporal.description: Speech perception and syllable segmentation dynamics

memory_temporal.description: Hippocampal sequence processing and temporal memory

papers.title: Temporal integration in human auditory cortex is predominantly yoked to absolute time
key_findings: Temporal integration throughout human auditory cortex is locked to absolute time (~5% variation)
Integration does not vary with speech structure duration (phonemes/words)
Uses intracranial recordings and time stretching/compression paradigm

papers.title: Multiscale temporal integration organizes hierarchical computation in human auditory cortex
key_findings: Integration windows span ~50 to 400 ms across cortical hierarchy
Hierarchical organization from primary to non-primary regions
Method developed for estimating sensory integration windows

papers.title: Neurons in auditory cortex integrate information within a constrained and context-invariant temporal window
key_findings: Ferret auditory cortex neurons have time-limited integration windows (15-150ms)
Windows increase from primary to non-primary auditory cortex
Integration is context-invariant

papers.title: A hierarchy of processing complexity and timescales for natural sounds in the human auditory cortex
key_findings: Processing timescales increase across auditory hierarchy
Complexity of cortical representations varies with temporal scale
Longer latencies for complex feature representations

papers.title: Temporal recurrence as a general mechanism to explain neural responses in the auditory system
key_findings: Deep recurrent neural networks outperform CNNs/Transformers for auditory cortex modeling
Auditory neurons have longer memory (several seconds) than STRF methods suggest
Temporal recurrence is key computational principle

papers.title: Processing Timescales as an Organizing Principle for Primate Cortex
key_findings: Hierarchical temporal receptive windows across cortex
Milliseconds in early sensory cortex to minutes in association areas
Temporal hierarchy parallels anatomical hierarchy

papers.title: Spectrotemporal modulation provides a unifying framework for auditory cortical asymmetries
key_findings: Left lateralization for temporal modulations
Right lateralization for spectral modulations
Spectrotemporal processing asymmetry explains hemispheric specialization

papers.title: Distinct nonlinear spectrotemporal integration in primary and secondary auditory cortex
key_findings: A1 shows temporally-asymmetric spectrotemporal integration
A2 shows symmetrical coincidence-preferring integration
Parallel rather than sequential processing between A1 and A2

papers.title: Beta-Band Oscillations Represent Auditory Beat and Its Metrical Hierarchy in Perception and Imagery
key_findings: β-band (13-30 Hz) oscillations in auditory and sensorimotor cortices track beat
Metrical hierarchy represented in β modulation patterns
Present in both perception and imagery conditions

papers.title: Selective Neuronal Entrainment to the Beat and Meter Embedded in a Musical Rhythm
key_findings: Steady-state evoked potentials (SS-EPs) at beat and meter frequencies
Selective enhancement at beat frequencies even without predominant acoustic energy
Spontaneous emergence of beat/meter from neural entrainment

papers.title: Neural Entrainment to the Beat: The Missing-Pulse Phenomenon
key_findings: Beat perception requires abstraction from temporal stimulus structure
Nonlinear neural interactions produce oscillations at beat frequency
Entrainment persists even when beat is acoustically absent

papers.title: Rhythmic Entrainment Echoes in Auditory Perception
key_findings: Entrainment echoes outlast rhythmic stimulation
Strongest echoes at 6 and 8 Hz
Evidence for endogenous oscillation entrainment

papers.title: Neural synchronization is strongest to the spectral flux of slow music and depends on familiarity and beat salience
key_findings: Strongest neural synchronization to spectral flux
Beat salience and familiarity modulate synchronization
Spectral flux better predictor than amplitude envelope

papers.title: Phase Entrainment of Human Delta Oscillations Can Mediate the Effects of Expectation on Reaction Speed
key_findings: Delta rhythm entrains to predictable target onset
Phase synchronization increases with expectation
Reaction times correlate with delta phase at target

papers.title: Causal links between parietal alpha activity and spatial auditory attention
key_findings: Alpha (10 Hz) tACS of right parietal cortex affects left auditory attention
Theta (6 Hz) stimulation has no effect
Causal role of parietal alpha in spatial auditory attention

papers.title: Cross-Modal Phase Reset Predicts Auditory Task Performance in Humans
key_findings: Visual input resets phase of auditory cortex oscillations
Phase reset at low alpha (8-10 Hz) and theta (~7 Hz)
Cross-modal phase reset predicts auditory performance

papers.title: The gamma rhythm as a guardian of brain health
key_findings: Gamma (30-150 Hz) implicated in feature binding and perception
GENUS: gamma entrainment using sensory stimulation
40 Hz stimulation shows therapeutic potential

papers.title: Cortical oscillations and speech processing: emerging computational principles and operations
key_findings: Delta, theta, gamma track speech at multiple timescales
Theta (~4-8 Hz) tracks syllable rate
Gamma encodes phonemic features

papers.title: 40 Hz Auditory Steady-State Response Is a Pharmacodynamic Biomarker for Cortical NMDA Receptors
key_findings: 40 Hz ASSR reflects NMDA receptor function
Gamma oscillations depend on excitatory/inhibitory balance
Schizophrenia shows reduced 40 Hz ASSR

papers.title: The auditory cortex mediates the perceptual effects of acoustic temporal expectation
key_findings: Temporal expectation enhances A1 neuron responses
Changes in neuronal representation predict performance
Early sensory cortex mediates expectation effects

papers.title: Language prediction mechanisms in human auditory cortex
key_findings: Two predictive mechanisms in early auditory cortex
Low-frequency phase predicts acoustic event timing
Planum temporale shows speech-specific prediction

papers.title: Temporally precise movement-based predictions in the mouse auditory cortex
key_findings: Movement signals in deep layers of auditory cortex
Temporal expectation peaks at expected feedback time
Internal model links actions to acoustic outcomes

papers.title: A Neuronal Model of Predictive Coding Accounting for the Mismatch Negativity
key_findings: MMN reflects prediction error in hierarchical model
Auditory system generates predictions about stimuli
Bottom-up and top-down message passing

papers.title: Ketamine Affects Prediction Errors about Statistical Regularities
key_findings: Ketamine reduces MMN amplitude
NMDA receptors critical for prediction error computation
Hierarchical Bayesian inference in auditory processing

papers.title: Novelty Detection in the Human Auditory Brainstem
key_findings: Deviance-related activity as early as 30-40 ms
Brainstem shows novelty detection
Prediction starts subcortically

papers.title: The neural bases for timing of durations
key_findings: Prospective vs retrospective timing engage different processes
Population state dynamics underlie duration encoding
Multiple brain regions contribute to timing

papers.title: The Neural Basis of Timing: Distributed Mechanisms for Diverse Functions
key_findings: Different mechanisms for different timescales
Sparse temporal selectivity in sensory cortex (10s-100s ms)
Basal ganglia for 100s ms to seconds

papers.title: Timing in the Absence of Clocks: Encoding Time in Neural Network States
key_findings: Millisecond timing doesn't rely on clock-like mechanisms
Time encoded in network state dynamics
Distinct mechanisms for ms vs second timing

papers.title: Neural representation of time in cortico-basal ganglia circuits
key_findings: Striatal neurons encode elapsed time
Time cells fire at specific intervals
Cortico-striatal circuits for interval timing

papers.title: Interval time coding by neurons in the presupplementary and supplementary motor areas
key_findings: Pre-SMA neurons code for different time intervals
Time-selective initiation of action
Activity represents passage of time

papers.title: Double dissociation of single-interval and rhythmic temporal prediction in cerebellar degeneration and Parkinson's disease
key_findings: Basal ganglia essential for rhythmic timing
Cerebellum essential for single-interval timing
Double dissociation between structures

papers.title: Integrator dynamics in the cortico-basal ganglia loop for flexible motor timing
key_findings: Ramping activity in frontal cortex and striatum
Variable ramp slopes anticipate movement onset
Cortico-basal ganglia loop as adjustable timer

papers.title: Dopamine transients follow a striatal gradient of reward time horizons
key_findings: DA dynamics differ across striatal subregions
Ventral to dorsolateral gradient in temporal integration
Different time horizons for reward prediction

papers.title: The temporal precision of reward prediction in dopamine neurons
key_findings: Dopamine neurons activated when reward exceeds prediction
Temporal precision declines with interval duration
Fixed-duration intervals 1-16s studied

papers.title: Feeling the Beat: Premotor and Striatal Interactions in Musicians and Nonmusicians during Beat Perception
key_findings: Dorsal premotor cortex, PFC, IPL, cerebellum active during beat perception
Functional overlap between perception and motor timing networks
Musicians show enhanced putamen activation

papers.title: β Oscillations Are Linked to the Initiation of Sensory-Cued Movement Sequences and the Internal Guidance of Regular Tapping in the Monkey
key_findings: β oscillations higher during internally-guided tapping
Putamen shows β entrainment during rhythmic tasks
Importance of internalized timing for movement

papers.title: Beta phase synchronization in the frontal-temporal-cerebellar network during auditory-to-motor rhythm learning
key_findings: Beta oscillations connect temporal and cerebellar areas
Phase synchronization during rhythm learning
Network-wide beta coordination

papers.title: Neural signals regulating motor synchronization in the primate deep cerebellar nuclei
key_findings: Dentate nucleus neurons track rhythmic structure
Internal model of rhythm for predictive control
Cerebellar modules for rhythm acquisition

papers.title: Secondary auditory cortex mediates a sensorimotor mechanism for action timing
key_findings: A2 mediates action timing in operant tasks
Sensorimotor mechanism revealed by optogenetics
Computational modeling of timing circuits

papers.title: Segregation of complex acoustic scenes based on temporal coherence
key_findings: Temporal coherence model explains stream segregation
Psychophysical data consistent with coherence model
Integration across widely spaced frequency channels

papers.title: Speech Categorization Reveals the Role of Early-Stage Temporal-Coherence Processing in Auditory Scene Analysis
key_findings: Temporal coherence processing may start at cochlear nucleus
Early neural basis for scene analysis
Speech categorization reveals coherence mechanisms

papers.title: Evidence for Neural Computations of Temporal Coherence in an Auditory Scene and Their Enhancement during Active Listening
key_findings: Neural tracking of coherence varies with attention
Larger temporal response functions during active listening
Top-down attention enhances coherence computation

papers.title: Competing Streams at the Cocktail Party: Exploring the Mechanisms of Attention and Temporal Integration
key_findings: Temporal integration critical for complex scene analysis
Bottom-up and top-down attention interact
Parietal cortex integrates auditory and attention signals

papers.title: Combining predictive coding and neural oscillations enables online syllable recognition in natural speech
key_findings: Theta-gamma coupling parses syllables
Phonemes encoded hierarchically within syllables
Online syllable recognition mechanism

papers.title: Oscillatory phase shapes syllable perception
key_findings: Theta phase (4-9 Hz) shapes syllable perception
Phase difference between /da/ and /ga/ percepts
Theta important for speech encoding

papers.title: Phonemic segmentation of narrative speech in human cerebral cortex
key_findings: Cortical entrainment at syllable rate
Phrase and sentence timescales in STG/IFG
Phoneme-level processing in auditory cortex

papers.title: Speech encoding by coupled cortical theta and gamma oscillations
key_findings: Theta tracks syllables in spoken language
Gamma encodes specific phoneme features
Cross-frequency coupling establishes phoneme sequence

papers.title: Replay-triggered brain-wide activation in humans
key_findings: Replay events reactivate past memories in sequence
Hippocampus and mPFC show heightened activity during replay
Replay strengthens hippocampus-DMN connectivity

papers.title: Spatiotemporal brain hierarchies of auditory memory recognition and predictive coding
key_findings: Feedforward connections from auditory cortex to hippocampus
Backward connections for predictive coding
Hierarchical processing for auditory memory

papers.title: Mnemonic construction and representation of temporal structure in the hippocampal formation
key_findings: Hippocampus represents temporal relations of learned sequences
Relational memories arise through mnemonic construction
Generalization reflects temporal event structure

papers.title: Hippocampal replay in the awake state: a potential substrate for memory consolidation and retrieval
key_findings: Replay is sequential reactivation of place cells
Occurs frequently in awake state
Substrate for memory consolidation and retrieval

papers.title: Thalamocortical loops as temporal demodulators across senses
key_findings: Thalamocortical circuits as oscillations-based phase-locked loops
Translate temporal code to rate code
Common mechanism across sensory modalities

papers.title: Involvement of the Thalamocortical Loop in the Spontaneous Switching of Percepts in Auditory Streaming
key_findings: MGB activity precedes perceptual switches
Asymmetric roles of MGB and AC in perception
Feedforward and feedback in thalamocortical loop

papers.title: The Thalamocortical Mechanism Underlying the Generation and Regulation of the Auditory Steady-State Responses in Awake Mice
key_findings: MGB and AC both contribute to ASSR
Thalamocortical synchronization for steady-state responses
Awake vs anesthetized differences

papers.title: Timing of Speech in Brain and Glottis and the Feedback Delay Problem in Motor Control
key_findings: Motor-sensory feedback has variable timing
Recalibration of auditory-motor mismatch threshold
Plastic predictions of peripheral timing

papers.title: Multiple processes of vocal sensory-motor interaction in primate auditory cortex
key_findings: Two timescales of vocal suppression: phasic and tonic
Corollary discharge predicts vocal timing and acoustics
Distinct suppression and enhancement mechanisms

papers.title: Identification of a motor-to-auditory pathway important for vocal learning
key_findings: Motor neurons transmit vocal signals to auditory cortex
Circuit essential for vocal imitation
Adaptive modification of vocal timing

papers.title: Single-Trial Speech Suppression of Auditory Cortex Activity in Humans
key_findings: Auditory cortex suppressed during vocalization
Efference copy from motor cortex
Single-trial suppression measurable

papers.title: Alpha Oscillations Related to Anticipatory Attention Follow Temporal Expectations
key_findings: Alpha power modulated by temporal expectations
Anticipatory attention indexed by alpha
Temporal cues guide attention allocation

papers.title: Temporal Expectation Improves the Quality of Sensory Information
key_findings: Temporal expectation enhances sensory quality
Not just faster responses but better perception
Gain modulation mechanism

papers.title: Musical Training Facilitates Exogenous Temporal Attention via Delta Phase Entrainment within a Sensorimotor Network
key_findings: Musicians show enhanced delta entrainment

Sensorimotor network involvement

papers.title: Encoding of Temporal Probabilities in the Human Brain
key_findings: Temporal probability encoded throughout cortical hierarchy
Includes early visual cortex
Hazard rate representation in brain

papers.title: Population clocks: motor timing with neural dynamics
key_findings: Time encoded in population activity patterns
Emerges from recurrent network dynamics
Accounts for many motor timing features

papers.title: Synaptic basis of a sub-second representation of time in a neural circuit model
key_findings: Sub-second timing through synaptic mechanisms
Ramping and time cell mechanisms
Circuit model for temporal representation

papers.title: The brain and its time: intrinsic neural timescales are key for input processing
key_findings: Different brain regions have different intrinsic timescales
Intrinsic neural timescales (INT) concept
Timescales shape input processing

papers.title: A model of temporal scaling correctly predicts that motor timing improves with speed
key_findings: Temporal scaling model for motor timing
Faster movements have better timing
Weber's law violations explained

r0_integration.description: H⁰ provides temporal grounding for R⁰ resonance features

multi_scale_integration.description: Implement hierarchical temporal integration as per cortical hierarchy
multi_scale_integration.implementation: Parallel processing streams with different integration windows

oscillation_based_timing.description: Use neural oscillation principles for temporal encoding
oscillation_based_timing.implementation: Phase-coded timing with delta/theta/beta frequency bands

predictive_processing.description: Implement prediction error computation for temporal expectations
predictive_processing.implementation: Hierarchical predictive model with bottom-up/top-down messages

population_dynamics.description: Time encoded in population activity trajectories
population_dynamics.implementation: RNN-based state evolution for temporal representation

key_insights_for_src9.1: Temporal integration is predominantly yoked to ABSOLUTE time, not structure duration
key_insights_for_src9.2: Cortical hierarchy shows systematic increase in integration windows (50ms → 400ms+)
key_insights_for_src9.3: Beta oscillations (13-30Hz) are critical for beat tracking and motor timing
key_insights_for_src9.4: Basal ganglia for rhythmic timing, cerebellum for single-interval timing (double dissociation)
key_insights_for_src9.5: Theta-gamma coupling enables hierarchical speech/music parsing
key_insights_for_src9.6: Population clocks encode time through network state dynamics
key_insights_for_src9.7: Dopamine signals follow striatal gradient of reward time horizons
key_insights_for_src9.8: Motor-auditory circuits use efference copies for predictive timing
key_insights_for_src9.9: Temporal coherence is the core principle for auditory scene analysis
